title: Bridge Paper: Physiological and Behavioral Synchrony Predict Group Cohesion and Performance.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects Ritual with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Ritual - neural synchrony in joint action, music entrainment, collective bonding.
**Phase context:** neural synchrony, hyperscanning, interpersonal synchronization

Paper: Physiological and Behavioral Synchrony Predict Group Cohesion and Performance.
DOI: 10.1038/s41598-020-65670-1
Abstract: Interpersonal synchrony contributes to social functioning in dyads, but it remains unknown how synchrony shapes group experiences and performance. To this end, we designed a novel group drumming task in which participants matched their drumming to either predictable or unpredictable tempos. Fifty-one three-person groups were randomly assigned to one of two conditions: synchronized or asynchronized drumming. Outcome measures included electrocardiograms and self-reports of group cohesion and synch
